metric_value: Inevitable adoption predicted
notes: Aberdeen asserts IRP adoption is inevitable; convergence of OOT/Internet/NCC makes it a certainty

metric_value: Partially realized as cloud ERP/SaaS
notes: IRP brand never took hold but concepts materialized as Salesforce/SAP ByDesign/NetSuite cloud ERP and supply chain platforms by 2005-2015

metric_value: Internet will be first business context for OO line-of-business adoption
notes: Aberdeen predicts Internet provides business context for OO deployment

metric_value: Confirmed: J2EE/web services became dominant enterprise OO platform by 2000-2005
notes: Java EE and web services (SOAP/REST) confirmed Internet as the OO enterprise application context

metric_value: Ready for enterprise deployment via Internet context
notes: Aberdeen finds OOT now deployable in enterprise via Internet; cites systems/network mgmt as first technical context

metric_value: Reaching market; enabling distributed component apps
notes: OLE Automation/OCX/ActiveX cited as long-anticipated distributed interprocess communication tech

metric_value: Reaching market alongside Java
notes: CORBA cited alongside Java and ActiveX as enabling distributed component applications

metric_value: Reaching market; enabling on-the-fly distributed component apps
notes: Java specifically cited as key enabler of distributed component-based applications

metric_name: Key enterprise challenge - supply chain dispersion
metric_value: Geographic dispersion and multi-pronged supply chain complexity is primary driver
notes: Supply chain complexity cited as core business driver for IRP adoption

metric_value: Interdependence of supply chain partners to reduce WIP inventory and cycle time pressure
notes: Inventory reduction and cycle time cited as economic drivers of IRP

metric_value: Best-of-breed definition shifting from vertical function to horizontal process
notes: Aberdeen: AP (vertical) evolving to Procurement (horizontal) as new definition of best-of-breed

metric_value: All enterprise app suppliers will remake products with IRP architecture
notes: Aberdeen believes suppliers must rethink products/functionality/architecture

metric_value: Confirmed: SAP NetWeaver/ESA (2003) and Salesforce (1999) represented architectural remake of EBA
notes: SAP NetWeaver (2004) and web-based ERP vendors confirmed architectural transformation of enterprise apps

metric_value: Real-time demand-side orders and near-immediate supply-side materials access achievable
notes: Aberdeen asserts APET enables supply chain visibility long sought but never attained

metric_value: Substantially realized through SAP APO/i2 Technologies/JDA by 2005-2010
notes: Advanced planning systems (i2 Technologies, SAP APO, Oracle ASCP) confirmed APET vision

metric_value: IRP makes virtual/networked corporation technically and operationally feasible
notes: Aberdeen predicts IRP enables true virtual corporation across supply chain

metric_value: Largely realized through SaaS platforms; Alibaba/Amazon supply networks exemplify virtual corporation
notes: Platform economy and e-commerce supply networks realized virtual corporation concept

metric_value: IRP enables no-maintenance thin clients hiding existing system complexity
notes: Thin clients cited as key IRP benefit reducing IT maintenance burden

metric_value: Browser-based SaaS fulfilled thin client vision; traditional thin client (Citrix/NC) failed
notes: Web browsers became the thin client; dedicated thin client hardware largely obsolete by 2010

metric_value: High-value lesson from BPR: enterprise apps must be cross-functional not stovepiped
notes: Aberdeen cites BPR experience as validating cross-functional application design

metric_value: BPR exercises proved operations are cross-functional; applications must match
notes: Retrospective finding from Aberdeen research on BPR outcomes

metric_value: IRP enables B2B e-commerce foundation for virtual corporation operations
notes: Aberdeen predicts IRP as foundation for B2B e-commerce

metric_value: Confirmed: EDI evolved to XML/web services B2B; Ariba/Commerce One built on these concepts 1999-2002
notes: B2B e-commerce boom 1999-2002 confirmed direction; Ariba/Commerce One B2B exchanges directly implemented IRP vision

metric_value: Leading enterprises will fundamentally alter best practices to adopt IRP
notes: Aberdeen predicts competitive pressure forces enterprise adoption

metric_value: Enterprises are on the cusp of watershed technology convergence event
notes: Aberdeen characterizes 1997 as inflection point for enterprise computing
